biologyPathway.title: Barrier Dysfunction
biologyPathway.short: The skin barrier loses moisture and protection.
biologyPathway.detail: Eczema often involves a weakened skin barrier, dryness, cracking, and increased sensitivity to irritants or allergens.

biologyPathway.title: Immune Inflammation
biologyPathway.detail: Inflammatory signaling can drive redness, swelling, itching, oozing, scaling, thickening, and flares.

biologyPathway.title: Itch-Scratch Loop
biologyPathway.detail: Itching leads to scratching, scratching damages skin, damaged skin becomes itchier. The loop is stupidly efficient.

biologyPathway.title: Trigger and Infection Screen
biologyPathway.detail: Soaps, fragrance, sweat, weather, allergens, stress, fabrics, infection, and irritants can worsen flares.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Eczema Is a Skin-Barrier Condition
body: Eczema/atopic dermatitis is medically a chronic inflammatory skin condition.
It involves skin-barrier weakness, dryness, itching, immune activation, genetics, irritants, allergens, weather, stress, and infection risk.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Itch and Inflammation
body: The skin barrier can lose moisture and become easier to irritate.
The immune system reacts, creating itch, redness, scaling, cracking, oozing, or thickened skin.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Care Basics
body: Gentle cleansing, fragrance-free moisturizers, trigger reduction, and clinician-guided anti-inflammatory treatment can help.
Scratching feels satisfying for three seconds and then ruins the meeting. Skin biology, a petty tyrant.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: Seek care for oozing, yellow crust, spreading redness, warmth, fever, severe pain, eye involvement, widespread rash, or sleep disruption.
Severe or infected eczema is not a character-building exercise.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Embassy With Thin Walls
paragraphs: Your skin is an embassy in a country that keeps getting invaded.
The walls are thin. The gate is glitchy. Border patrol has been awake since 2012 and now suspects everything, including laundry detergent and emotional labor.
The invaders are fragrance, sweat, wool, stress, weather, allergens, and the ninth person this week asking for just one quick favor.
The ambassador, your nervous system, keeps sending memos that say EVERYTHING IS FINE while the lobby is on fire.
The itch is the alarm. The scratch is the badly trained guard firing into the ceiling.
The embassy needs a gate, a moisturizer budget, and fewer people wandering through like your boundaries are a public park.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Boundary Overload Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a skin problem. You may also have a boundary-overload pattern.
Medical first: eczema is barrier dysfunction and inflammation. Moisturizers, triggers, prescription treatment, and infection checks matter.
Symbolically, though, ask who or what keeps getting under your skin.
What obligation, person, fabric, soap, stressor, or old wound keeps scratching at the same place?
Your sensitivity is not weakness. It is data with nerve endings.
Soothe the barrier. Reduce the irritants. Set the boundary before your skin has to scream it.
Build the gate or keep letting every invader scratch at the embassy walls. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Lung Skin Boundary
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Lung is associated with skin, surface defense, and boundary.
Symbolically, eczema can mirror a protective layer that feels overworked and under-resourced.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen Processing

When worry and overwhelm accumulate, the symbolic skin can feel damp, irritated, and overloaded.

influenceLayers.title: Thin Skin Archetype
paragraphs: The Thin Skin archetype is not weakness.
It asks for smarter protection, not self-hatred.

influenceLayers.title: Itch as No
paragraphs: The itch can symbolize something that feels intrusive.
The work is learning the no before the scratch.

influenceLayers.title: Gate Repair
paragraphs: The reset is barrier repair, trigger reduction, nervous-system downshifting, and clearer boundaries.
The embassy needs maintenance, not punishment.

reset.title: Skin Boundary Soothing & Embassy Gate Reset
steps: Sit upright and place warm hands over the upper chest or near the flare without scratching.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I am allowed to protect my boundaries. I do not have to let everything in.
Then do one skin-friendly action: moisturize, remove an irritant, cool the area, or write down a trigger.

biologyPath.title: Boundary Breach
biologyPath.description: You allow someone or something to cross your emotional perimeter, causing silent resentment.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Failing to establish a verbal boundary signals to your amygdala that your protective field is compromised.

biologyPath.title: CRH Release
biologyPath.description: Stress hormones are released directly into the local microenvironment of the skin.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Hypothalamic CRH enters the bloodstream and binds directly to receptors on epidermal mast cells.

biologyPath.title: Mast Degranulation
biologyPath.description: Skin immune cells rupture, dumping a flood of histamines and inflammatory chemicals.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Degranulation triggers local vasodilation, swelling, and intense stimulation of C-fiber nerve endings (the itch signal).

biologyPath.title: Barrier Collapse
biologyPath.description: Scratching can degrade the skin's protective lipophilic layer, leaving it dry and vulnerable.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Physical trauma to the stratum corneum allows moisture to escape and external irritants to penetrate, worsening the local response.

biologyPath.title: Inflammatory Flare
biologyPath.description: The skin erupts in red, dry, itchy plaques that scream for distance and protection.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: The skin acts as a literal barrier of defense, erupting in inflammation to force you to pull back from the world.

biologyPathway.title: Sebum and Follicle Clogging
biologyPathway.short: Oil and dead skin block pores.
biologyPathway.detail: Acne often starts when oil and dead skin cells clog hair follicles.

biologyPathway.title: Hormones and Genetics
biologyPathway.detail: Hormonal shifts can increase oil production. Genetics can affect susceptibility, because apparently your pores also inherited family drama.

biologyPathway.title: Bacteria and Inflammation
biologyPathway.short: C. acnes and immune response amplify lesions.
biologyPathway.detail: Cutibacterium acnes can contribute to inflammation inside clogged follicles, leading to papules, pustules, nodules, or cysts.

biologyPathway.title: Skin Barrier and Triggers
biologyPathway.short: Products, picking, meds, and stress can worsen it.
biologyPathway.detail: Harsh scrubbing, pore-clogging products, picking, some medications, stress, and certain dietary patterns may worsen acne in some people.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Acne Is a Skin Condition
body: Acne is medically an inflammatory skin condition involving hair follicles and oil glands.
It is not caused by being dirty, morally defective, or insufficiently committed to face wash capitalism.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Oil, Cells, and Hormones
body: Sebum, dead skin cells, follicle clogging, hormones, genetics, and skin turnover can all contribute.
Androgens can increase oil production, and some people are simply more acne-prone.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Bacteria and Inflammation
body: Cutibacterium acnes can grow in clogged follicles and trigger inflammation.
This can create blackheads, whiteheads, papules, pustules, nodules, cysts, redness, pain, and sometimes scarring.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Care and Escalation
body: Gentle skin care, avoiding picking, and evidence-based acne treatment matter.
Painful cysts, scarring, severe acne, infection signs, sudden adult onset, or acne with irregular periods/excess hair growth should be evaluated.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Wastewater Treatment Plant
paragraphs: Your face is a wastewater treatment plant with clogged pipes and one flickering light over the control panel.
The sebaceous glands are the drain pipes. They make oil, which is useful until the system gets backed up.
Dead skin cells join the party. Hormones turn up the oil supply. C. acnes moves in like it owns the place.
Your immune system arrives as the hazmat crew, late, undertrained, and far too enthusiastic about inflammation.
Now the pipes are red, swollen, and angry. The break room has a HANG IN THERE poster and a mug that says WORLD'S OKAYEST FILTER.
The goal is not to punish the plant. The goal is unclog, calm, and stop setting fires to fix plumbing.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Real Face Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a skin problem. You may also have a hidden-face pattern.
Acne is not dirty. Acne is not failure. Acne is inflammation, oil, clogged follicles, bacteria, hormones, genetics, products, meds, and sometimes stress having a group project on your face.
Stop scrubbing like punishment is skincare. Stop picking like excavation will reveal inner peace.
Symbolically, acne can ask what is trying to surface.
What anger, grief, embarrassment, or truth keeps pushing through the mask?
Treat the skin gently. Get real treatment when needed. Let your face be a face, not a courtroom exhibit.
Show the real face or keep letting your pores file complaints. Your choice.

Symbolically, acne can mirror boundary stress surfacing at the place the world sees.

The skin needs treatment, not war.

Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: This face is allowed to feel what it feels. I care for my skin without attacking myself.

biologyPathway.title: Hair Cycle Shift
biologyPathway.short: Hair follicles cycle through growth and rest.
biologyPathway.detail: Hair loss can happen when too many follicles enter shedding, when follicles miniaturize, or when inflammation damages the scalp.

biologyPathway.title: Genetics and Androgens
biologyPathway.detail: Androgenetic alopecia involves genetic sensitivity to androgen signaling, often causing patterned thinning.

biologyPathway.title: Stress/Illness Shedding
biologyPathway.short: Telogen effluvium can follow a trigger.
biologyPathway.detail: Major stress, illness, surgery, weight loss, medication changes, or deficiency can push follicles into a shedding phase.

biologyPathway.title: Medical Rule-Outs
biologyPathway.short: Some hair loss needs labs or dermatology.
biologyPathway.detail: Thyroid disease, iron deficiency, autoimmune alopecia areata, scalp infection, inflammatory scalp disease, and medications can contribute.

clinical.label: Strictly Medical
clinical.title: Hair Cycle, Genetics & Scalp Health Cascade

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Many Types of Hair Loss
body: Hair loss is not one thing.

Possible causes include androgenetic alopecia, genetics, aging, telogen effluvium, thyroid disease, iron or nutrient deficiency, autoimmune alopecia areata, medications, scalp disease, traction, illness, and stress.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Pattern Matters
body: Patterned thinning, diffuse shedding, patchy bald spots, scalp pain, scaling, scarring, and hairline recession point to different causes.
This is why guessing from vibes is bad medicine, even if the vibes have confidence.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Resources and Follicles
body: Hair growth depends on follicle cycling, hormones, nutrition, scalp health, inflammation status, and overall health.
Stress can contribute to shedding in some cases, but it is not the only cause and does not erase genetics.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Care Triggers
body: Sudden patchy loss, rapid unexplained shedding, scalp pain, scaling, rash, scarring, infection signs, or systemic symptoms deserves medical/dermatology evaluation.
Early treatment may matter depending on cause.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Underfunded School District
paragraphs: Your hair follicles are a school district that just lost funding.
The teachers are nutrients, blood flow, hormones, scalp health, and sleep. Half of them are exhausted. One is crying in the copier room.
The administrators, cortisol and genetics, keep sending memos about resource allocation while the classrooms empty.
Some students drop out from androgen sensitivity. Some leave after illness or stress. Some get evicted by autoimmune nonsense. The district is complicated, because biology hates clean storylines.
There is a sign that says WE'RE DOING OUR BEST. Someone wrote THEN FUND THE ROOTS underneath.
That means data, labs when needed, scalp care, medical guidance, rest, and not buying snake-oil serum from a man yelling in a tank top.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Resource Retreat Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have a hair-loss problem. You may also have a resource-depletion pattern.
Do not pretend it is all stress. Genetics, hormones, thyroid, iron, meds, autoimmune disease, scalp disease, and aging all get a vote.
Symbolically, though, hair loss can feel like vitality retreating from the surface.
Where are you running on empty? Where are you performing strength while your system cuts funding to nonessential departments?
Get facts. Track timing. Check scalp symptoms. Review meds. Consider labs or dermatology. Then deal with the survival mode that keeps draining you.
Your hair is not your worth. Your scalp is not a referendum on your value. Humanity needed this reminder, apparently.
Fund the roots or keep watching the district close schools. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Root Funding
paragraphs: The reset is replenishment plus evaluation.
Rest, nutrition, labs, scalp care, and appropriate treatment fund the roots.

reset.title: Root Replenishment & Survival Mode Downshift
steps: Sit upright and place one hand at the back of the neck near the hairline.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I am safe enough to rest. I am more than what I am losing.
After 2 minutes, choose one practical step: track shedding, review meds, schedule labs, improve sleep, or book dermatology.

biologyPathway.title: Skin Stress
biologyPathway.short: Friction, heat, infection, or inflammation triggers injury.
biologyPathway.detail: A blister forms when layers of skin separate and fluid collects between them.

biologyPathway.title: Fluid Pocket
biologyPathway.detail: The blister can protect tissue underneath while new skin forms. Annoying little waterbed, but useful.

biologyPathway.title: Cause Matters
biologyPathway.short: Not all blisters are the same.
biologyPathway.detail: Friction blisters differ from burns, herpes, shingles, impetigo, allergic dermatitis, autoimmune blistering, medication reactions, or diabetic wounds.

biologyPathway.title: Infection Screen
biologyPathway.detail: Pus, spreading redness, warmth, fever, severe pain, location near eyes/genitals/mouth, or widespread blistering changes the safety level.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Blister Formation
body: Blisters are fluid-filled pockets that form when skin layers separate.
Common causes include friction, burns, allergic/contact reactions, viral infections, bacterial infection, autoimmune disease, medication reactions, and medical conditions affecting skin healing.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Protective Bubble
body: A clean friction blister often acts as a protective cushion while new skin forms underneath.
Popping it can increase infection risk, because apparently the body did not design this as a party favor.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Cause Check
body: Location, pain, redness, drainage, fever, exposure, burn history, sexual exposure, shingles pattern, medications, diabetes, and immune status matter.
A shoe blister and a widespread medication reaction are not the same animal.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Red Flags
body: Seek care for spreading redness, warmth, pus, fever, severe pain, large burns, eye/mouth/genital blisters, diabetes, poor circulation, or widespread blistering.
Blisters can be simple. They can also be medical nonsense with a costume.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Pressure Cooker With a Stuck Valve
paragraphs: Your skin is a pressure cooker in a kitchen that has not passed inspection since 2009.
The burner is friction, heat, infection, allergy, or whatever tiny disaster got assigned to today's episode.
The valve is stuck. The gauge is red. The cook keeps saying it's fine while the lid hisses like a snake with paperwork.
The blister is a little steam dome where the system says: I cannot keep taking this pressure.
Sometimes the fix is padding and protection. Sometimes it is medical care. Sometimes it is not popping it like a raccoon with thumbs.
The valve needs safe release, not dramatic rupture.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Friction Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a blister problem. You may also have a pressure-release pattern.
Medical first: friction, burns, herpes, shingles, allergy, infection, autoimmune disease, meds, diabetes, and circulation issues all matter.
Symbolically, ask what keeps rubbing you raw.
What person, shoe, job, habit, or emotional loop keeps creating the same hot spot?
Your skin is not being dramatic. Repeated friction eventually makes a bubble. Humans needed a biology lesson to learn this. Inspiring.
Protect the spot. Reduce the rub. Check red flags. Express irritation before your skin turns it into a water balloon.
Open the valve safely or keep letting your skin blow the whistle. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Liver Heat
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Liver pattern is associated with frustration, heat, and blocked movement.

influenceLayers.title: Protect the Surface
paragraphs: The reset is protection, padding, cooling, and honest irritation.
The body is asking for less rubbing and more response.

reset.title: Skin Boundary Cooling & Pressure Release
steps: Sit upright and avoid pressing directly on the blister.
Place one hand near the irritated area without squeezing it.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds, imagining heat leaving the area.
Whisper: I protect this boundary. I release irritation safely.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Blisters with infection signs, burns, high-risk locations, diabetes, circulation problems, or widespread symptoms need medical care.

items.label: Protect
items.detail: Cover clean friction blisters with a protective dressing.

items.label: Reduce friction
items.detail: Change shoes, socks, tools, grip, padding, or repetitive rubbing sources.

items.label: Keep clean
items.detail: Clean surrounding skin gently and avoid picking.

items.label: Cause check
items.detail: Consider burns, allergy, herpes/shingles, medications, diabetes, or infection if the pattern is unusual.

avoid: Do not pop blisters unless medically directed.
Do not ignore spreading redness, warmth, pus, fever, severe pain, mouth/eye/genital blisters, large burns, diabetes wounds, or widespread blistering.

brutalActions: Stop the friction.
Protect the blister instead of picking at it like civilization failed.
Check for infection or serious causes.
Name one irritation before your skin has to stage a protest bubble.

clinical.mechanism: Blisters are fluid-filled pockets in the skin that can come from friction, burns, allergic/contact reactions, viral infections such as herpes or shingles, bacterial infection, autoimmune disease, medication reactions, diabetes-related wounds, or other skin conditions.
protocol: Protect clean friction blisters and reduce rubbing.
Avoid popping unless medically directed.
Seek care for infection signs, high-risk locations, diabetes/circulation concerns, severe pain, burns, or widespread blistering.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for medical evaluation.

brutal.realityCheck: You do have a blister problem. You may also have a pressure-release pattern.

Severe pain or rapidly worsening blister

hero.primaryPattern: Boils/furuncles are painful skin infections often involving hair follicles, Staph bacteria, pus formation, inflammation, and sometimes abscess development.
hero.safetyPreview: Fever, spreading redness, severe pain, face/spine location, large/recurrent boils, diabetes, immune suppression, or no improvement needs medical care.

biologyPathway.title: Follicle Infection
biologyPathway.detail: Boils often begin when bacteria enter a hair follicle or damaged skin.

biologyPathway.title: Inflammatory Response
biologyPathway.detail: White blood cells gather to fight infection, creating swelling, heat, redness, and pain.

biologyPathway.title: Pus Pocket
biologyPathway.detail: Pus is made of immune cells, bacteria, and tissue debris. Elegant? No. Effective? Sometimes.

biologyPathway.title: Complication Screen
biologyPathway.short: Some boils need drainage or antibiotics.
biologyPathway.detail: Large, recurrent, face/spine-area, feverish, spreading, or high-risk boils should be evaluated.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Boils Are Infections
body: A boil, or furuncle, is medically a skin infection, often involving a hair follicle.
Staphylococcus bacteria are a common cause. The result can be a painful, red, swollen lump that fills with pus.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Pus and Pressure
body: The immune system sends white blood cells to contain the infection.
As pus collects, pressure rises and the boil may become more painful or come to a head.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Care Basics
body: Warm compresses may help some boils drain naturally.
Squeezing, cutting, or bathroom-surgery nonsense can spread infection. Humans keep trying this anyway because thumbs were a mistake.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Medical Escalation
body: Seek care for fever, spreading redness, red streaks, severe pain, face/spine location, large or recurrent boils, diabetes, immune suppression, or a boil that does not improve.
Some boils need professional drainage or antibiotics.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Landfill Blowout
paragraphs: Your skin is a landfill that was closed in 1995 but never properly capped.
Garbage trucks kept arriving: bacteria, friction, sweat, shaving irritation, immune stress, and whatever emotional trash you keep pretending is compost.
The landfill operator says we're full. The trucks keep dumping anyway.
Eventually pressure finds a weak spot and blows upward. That is the boil.
The pus is not a personality flaw. It is the immune system's cleanup crew, very dead, very committed, and honestly underpaid.
The fix is not squeezing the volcano. The fix is warm compresses, medical care when needed, and cleaning the actual source.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Festering Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a boil problem. You may also have a letting-things-fester pattern.
Medical first: boils are infections. Staph exists. Fever, spreading redness, severe pain, and high-risk locations need care.
Symbolically, though, ask what you have been letting rot under the surface.
What resentment is swollen because you keep calling it fine? What boundary breach became normal? What conflict needs cleaning instead of avoidance?
Do not squeeze the boil and do not squeeze your life into silence.
Warm compress. Watch red flags. Get care when needed. Then stop letting old garbage live under the skin of your life.
Clean the landfill or keep letting the ground rupture. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Stomach Toxic Heat
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, boils can be framed as heat and toxicity pushing to the surface.
Symbolically, this can mirror resentment or toxic situations that have been held too long.

influenceLayers.title: Lung Skin Boundary
paragraphs: The Lung is associated with the skin and protective boundary.
A boil can symbolize a breached boundary becoming visible.

influenceLayers.title: Rotting Hoarder Archetype
paragraphs: The Purger knows what must be expelled.
When suppressed, waste becomes pressure instead of release.

influenceLayers.title: Festering Silence
paragraphs: This symptom can ask what has been ignored until it swelled.
The body hates neglected messes. It files them in pus, because apparently subtlety was unavailable.

influenceLayers.title: Clean the Source
paragraphs: The medical reset is infection care.
The symbolic reset is honest cleanup.

reset.title: Heat Drainage & Clean the Source Reset
steps: Do not press hard on the boil.
Sit comfortably with one hand near, not on, the affected area.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds, imagining pressure releasing safely.
Whisper: I am allowed to clean what has been festering. I do not have to carry what is toxic.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Boils can require medical drainage or antibiotics, especially with red flags or risk factors.

items.label: Warm compress
items.detail: Warm compresses may help some boils drain naturally.

items.label: Do not squeeze
items.detail: Squeezing can worsen inflammation or spread infection.

items.label: Keep clean
items.detail: Keep the area clean and covered as appropriate.

items.label: Medical care
items.detail: Large, recurrent, face/spine-area, feverish, spreading, or high-risk boils need evaluation.

avoid: Do not lance or squeeze boils at home.
Do not ignore fever, spreading redness, red streaks, severe pain, face/spine location, diabetes, immune suppression, or recurrent boils.

brutalActions: Use warm compresses, not bathroom surgery.
Do not squeeze it.
Get care for fever, spreading redness, severe pain, face/spine location, diabetes, immune suppression, or recurrent boils.
Name one thing you have been letting fester.

clinical.mechanism: Boils, also called furuncles, are painful skin infections involving a hair follicle or deeper skin tissue, often caused by Staphylococcus bacteria. They can fill with pus and may require medical drainage or antibiotics depending on severity.
protocol: Use warm compresses and keep the area clean.
Do not squeeze, cut, or lance boils at home.
Seek care for fever, spreading redness, severe pain, face/spine location, large/recurrent boils, diabetes, immune suppression, or lack of improvement.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for medical care.

brutal.realityCheck: You do have a boil problem. You may also have a letting-things-fester pattern.

Spreading redness, warmth, swelling, red streaks, or pus worsening
Severe pain, rapidly enlarging boil, or multiple boils
Boil on face, near nose, near eye, near spine, or over a joint
Diabetes, immune suppression, poor circulation, or recurrent boils
No improvement after several days or recurrent abscesses

hero.primaryPattern: Cold sores are HSV-related outbreaks that may involve prodrome tingling, burning, blisters, crusting, contagious shedding, and triggers like stress, illness, sun, hormones, or immune changes.
hero.safetyPreview: Eye symptoms, severe/frequent outbreaks, immune suppression, newborn exposure, widespread sores, or severe illness needs medical care.

biologyPathway.title: HSV Dormancy
biologyPathway.detail: After infection, herpes simplex virus can remain dormant in nerve tissue and reactivate later.

biologyPathway.title: Trigger and Prodrome
biologyPathway.detail: Stress, illness, sun exposure, hormonal shifts, injury, or immune changes can trigger reactivation. Tingling, itching, or burning may come before blisters.

biologyPathway.title: Blister and Shedding
biologyPathway.detail: Cold sores can blister, ulcerate, crust, and shed virus. Avoid direct contact during prodrome and outbreak.

biologyPathway.title: Risk Screen
biologyPathway.detail: Eye symptoms, severe outbreaks, frequent recurrence, immune suppression, newborn exposure, or widespread lesions require medical guidance.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — HSV First
body: Cold sores are medically caused by herpes simplex virus, usually HSV-1.
Stress may trigger outbreaks in some people, but stress is not the virus. Biology, somehow, insists on being specific.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Prodrome
body: Tingling, burning, itching, or tenderness can appear before the sore.
That prodrome stage can still be contagious, because viruses are rude little opportunists.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Outbreak Care
body: Antivirals work best when used early if prescribed by a clinician.
Avoid kissing, sharing drinks/utensils/lip products, touching the sore, and oral sex during prodrome or active outbreak.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Escalation Check
body: Seek care for eye symptoms, severe or frequent outbreaks, immune suppression, newborn exposure, widespread lesions, trouble swallowing, or severe illness.
Eye herpes is not a wait-and-see hobby.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Emergency Broadcast Lip System
paragraphs: Your lips are an emergency broadcast system that has been screaming THIS IS A TEST for too long.
The supervisors, Cortisol and Adrenaline, keep hammering the red button every time you swallow a sentence that needed daylight.
The technicians, your immune system, are tired. HSV sees the chaos and strolls in like it has backstage passes.
Then comes the buzz, the burn, the blister, and the tiny crusted press conference no one requested.
The sign says WE'RE FINE. Someone wrote THEN STOP PRESSING THE BUTTON underneath.
The broadcast system needs early antivirals when appropriate, less direct contact during outbreaks, and fewer unsaid words charging the wires.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Swallowed Speech Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a cold sore problem. You may also have a swallowed-speech pattern.
Medical first: HSV is real, contagious, and sometimes needs antivirals. Do not make your lips a public transit system for viral particles.
Symbolically, though, ask what keeps burning at your mouth.
What truth did you bite back? What boundary did you turn into a smile? What sentence keeps trying to escape as heat?
Speak earlier. Treat earlier. Avoid spreading it. Protect eyes and vulnerable people.
Your lip does not need to become an emergency broadcast tower for every unsaid no.
Speak before the broadcast system hijacks your lip again. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Liver Heat
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, Liver heat can symbolize frustration rising upward.
Cold sores can symbolically mirror heat reaching the mouth.

influenceLayers.title: Stomach Rumination
paragraphs: The Stomach pattern is linked with rumination and churning.
When words are repeatedly swallowed, the mouth becomes the symbolic pressure point.

influenceLayers.title: Burning Mouth Archetype
paragraphs: The Speaker says the sentence before it becomes heat.
When suppressed, the lips carry what the throat refused.

influenceLayers.title: Prodrome as Warning
paragraphs: The prodrome can become a symbolic early warning.
Something is building. Respond before eruption.

influenceLayers.title: Broadcast Repair
paragraphs: The reset is early response, lower stress load, and cleaner speech.
The broadcast system does not need another false alarm.

reset.title: Lip Boundary & Broadcast System Reset
steps: Sit upright and avoid touching the sore.
Place one hand on the chest and one near the mouth without contact.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I can speak what burns before it erupts. I protect myself and others.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. HSV outbreaks may need antiviral medication and medical guidance.

items.label: Early treatment
items.detail: Clinician-prescribed antivirals work best when started early.

items.label: Avoid spread
items.detail: Avoid kissing, oral sex, sharing drinks/utensils/lip products, and touching lesions during prodrome/outbreak.

items.label: Sun protection
items.detail: Lip SPF may reduce sun-triggered outbreaks for some people.

items.label: Trigger tracking
items.detail: Track stress, illness, sun, sleep, hormones, and irritation.

avoid: Do not touch the sore then touch your eyes.
Do not ignore eye symptoms, severe/frequent outbreaks, immune suppression, newborn exposure, widespread lesions, or severe illness.

brutalActions: Do not spread it.
Use prescribed antivirals early when appropriate.
Protect eyes, newborns, and immunocompromised people.
Say one sentence you keep swallowing before your lip files another broadcast.

clinical.mechanism: Cold sores are usually caused by herpes simplex virus reactivation, often HSV-1. Outbreaks can involve tingling, burning, painful blisters, crusting, contagious viral shedding, and triggers such as stress, illness, sun exposure, hormonal shifts, or immune changes.
protocol: Consider clinician-guided antiviral treatment early if prescribed.
Avoid kissing, oral sex, sharing items, and touching lesions during prodrome/outbreak.
Seek care for eye symptoms, severe/frequent outbreaks, immune suppression, newborn exposure, widespread lesions, or severe illness.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for HSV diagnosis or medical care.

witty.metaphorText: Your lips are a glitchy emergency broadcast system that HSV hijacks when stress keeps hitting the test button.
witty.wittyAdvice: Treat early, do not spread it, and stop letting unsaid words charge the wires.

brutal.realityCheck: You do have a cold sore problem. You may also have a swallowed-speech pattern.

Severe, frequent, widespread, or unusually painful outbreaks
Fever, severe illness, trouble swallowing, dehydration, or inability to eat/drink

hero.primaryPattern: Gray hair may involve genetics, aging, reduced melanin production, oxidative stress, nutrition, thyroid issues, autoimmune conditions, smoking, medications, illness, or stress.
hero.safetyPreview: Sudden graying with fatigue, weight change, hair loss, vitiligo, thyroid symptoms, anemia symptoms, or autoimmune signs deserves medical review.

biologyPathway.title: Melanin Production
biologyPathway.detail: Hair color comes from melanin made by pigment cells in follicles. When pigment production declines, hair can grow gray or white.

biologyPathway.title: Genetics and Aging
biologyPathway.detail: Genetics and aging are major drivers of graying. Stress can be involved for some people, but it does not get to steal the whole microphone.

biologyPathway.title: Medical Contributors
biologyPathway.detail: Premature graying can sometimes be associated with nutritional deficiencies, thyroid disease, autoimmune conditions, vitiligo, smoking, medications, or illness.

biologyPathway.title: Meaning Layer
biologyPathway.detail: Symbolically, gray hair can feel like color draining after years of over-giving, survival mode, and unrefilled reserves.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Pigment Biology
body: Gray hair happens when hair follicles produce less pigment.
This can be normal aging, family genetics, oxidative stress, smoking, or other biological factors.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Premature Graying Check
body: Premature graying may be worth reviewing if it is sudden, early, or paired with other symptoms.
Possible contributors can include nutritional deficiency, thyroid disease, autoimmune conditions, vitiligo, illness, medications, or major stress.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Stress Without Fantasy Medicine
body: Stress may affect pigment biology in some cases, but gray hair should not be reduced to fear or depletion alone.
Genetics remains the annoying landlord of many hair stories.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Replenishment
body: Support means checking health basics, sleep, nutrition, stress load, and symptoms when appropriate.
There is no guaranteed natural reversal. The supplement aisle will survive this betrayal.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Art Studio With No Paint
paragraphs: Your hair follicles are an art studio that lost its paint budget.
The melanocytes are the paint supply. The hairs are blank canvases wandering into class asking why everything is monochrome.
The administrator keeps saying resources are limited while genetics quietly signs the purchase order.
The artist is tired. The brushes are dry. The paint store has a CLOSED FOR INVENTORY sign that has been up since 2005.
Some of this is biology. Some may be stress. Some may be nutrition or thyroid or autoimmune weirdness, because the body refuses to keep one clean plotline.
The fix is not panic. It is restock what can be restocked and stop treating silver like personal failure.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Paint Budget Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have a gray-hair concern. You may also have a depletion-and-visibility pattern.
Do not pretend stress is the only cause. Genetics, aging, nutrition, thyroid, autoimmune issues, smoking, illness, and pigment biology all get a vote.
Symbolically, though, ask where your color went.
What drained you? What made you feel safer fading into the background? What part of your life has been running in grayscale?
Replenish what can be replenished: sleep, food, labs if needed, rest, joy, sunlight that does not fry you, and one less unpaid emotional labor shift.
Gray is not failure. It can be aging, wisdom, biology, or the paint store asking for a budget meeting.
Restock the paint store or keep sending blank canvases down the line. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney Jing
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, Kidney Jing is linked with aging, hair, and deep reserves.
Symbolically, graying can mirror the feeling that reserves are running low.

influenceLayers.title: Vital Color
paragraphs: Color becomes a symbol of vitality and expression.
When vitality is hidden or drained, the image becomes fading.

influenceLayers.title: Bleached Identity
paragraphs: The gray can symbolize becoming less visible or less vibrant to stay safe.
The work is reclaiming visibility without pretending biology is optional.

influenceLayers.title: Resource Conservation
paragraphs: This layer asks where the system has cut beauty, color, or play to fund survival.
The art department needs a budget.

influenceLayers.title: Paint Restock
paragraphs: The reset is replenishment, health checks, and permission to be visible.
Not everything can be reversed. Some things can be respected.

reset.title: Paint Store Restock & Visibility Reset
steps: Sit upright with both hands on the lower back.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I am allowed to be full. I am allowed to be colorful. I am allowed to be seen.
Then choose one replenishing action: sleep, food, labs, sunlight safely, joy, or refusing one draining demand.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Premature or sudden graying may need medical review depending on age, symptoms, and context.

items.label: Health review
items.detail: Consider thyroid, B12, iron/ferritin, autoimmune symptoms, vitiligo, medications, illness, and family history.

items.label: Nutrition basics
items.detail: Support overall hair health with adequate protein, micronutrients, and medical guidance for deficiencies.

items.label: Stress recovery
items.detail: Sleep, rest, and stress reduction may support overall health even if they do not reverse gray hair.

items.label: Hair choice
items.detail: Coloring, embracing, trimming, or styling are valid. The mirror is not a courtroom.

avoid: Do not assume all graying is stress.
Do not trust miracle anti-gray supplements without evidence or medical guidance.

brutalActions: Check health factors if graying is sudden or early.
Stop blaming character defects for pigment biology.
Restock one depleted area of life.
Let silver exist without turning it into a felony.

clinical.mechanism: Gray or premature gray hair happens when hair follicles produce less pigment. It can involve genetics, aging, oxidative stress, smoking, nutritional deficiencies, thyroid disease, autoimmune conditions, vitiligo, medications, illness, or chronic stress in some cases.
protocol: Review timing, family history, stress, nutrition, smoking, illness, medications, and other symptoms.
Consider medical review for sudden, early, or symptom-associated graying.
Do not rely on miracle reversal claims.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for medical evaluation.

witty.metaphorTitle: The Art Studio With No Paint
witty.metaphorText: Your follicles are an art studio where the paint supply is low and the canvases are coming out silver.
witty.wittyAdvice: Restock what you can and stop suing your hair for aging.

brutal.realityCheck: You may have a gray-hair concern. You may also have a depletion-and-visibility pattern.
protocolSteps: Eat like pigment cells are employees.

Gray hair with hair loss, patchy loss, rash, scaling, or scalp symptoms

hero.primaryPattern: Hives may involve histamine, mast cells, allergy, infection, medications, foods, insect stings, pressure, temperature, exercise, stress, autoimmune patterns, or unknown triggers.
hero.safetyPreview: Trouble breathing, wheezing, throat/tongue/lip swelling, fainting, dizziness, vomiting, or anaphylaxis symptoms need emergency care.

biologyPathway.title: Mast Cell Activation
biologyPathway.detail: Hives happen when mast cells release histamine and other mediators, causing raised itchy welts.

biologyPathway.title: Trigger Variety
biologyPathway.detail: Foods, meds, infections, insect stings, temperature, pressure, exercise, stress, autoimmune issues, and unknown causes can all be involved.

biologyPathway.title: Migrating Welts
biologyPathway.detail: Welts may appear, fade, and reappear elsewhere. Very theatrical for a skin reaction.

biologyPathway.title: Anaphylaxis Screen
biologyPathway.detail: Breathing trouble, throat/tongue/lip swelling, wheezing, fainting, or severe systemic symptoms require emergency care.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Hives Are Histamine Welts
body: Hives, or urticaria, are medically raised itchy welts caused by histamine/mast-cell activation.
They can be allergic, infection-related, medication-related, physical-triggered, autoimmune-linked, stress-amplified, or idiopathic.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Triggers
body: Triggers can include foods, medications, insect stings, infections, heat, cold, pressure, exercise, stress, or unknown causes.
The body loves mystery alarms. Very efficient. Very annoying.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Migration Pattern
body: Hives often move, fade, and reappear in different places.
That migration does not automatically mean danger, but it does mean tracking timing and triggers can help.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Emergency Check
body: Trouble breathing, wheezing, throat/tongue/lip swelling, fainting, dizziness, vomiting, or anaphylaxis symptoms need emergency care.
Do not turn airway swelling into a breathing exercise. Humanity, please.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Security System Set to Extreme
paragraphs: Your skin is a security system set to EXTREME SENSITIVITY.
A dust mite walks by and the alarm starts screaming. Laundry detergent changes brands and the floodlights deploy.
The technicians, your immune system, are exhausted from chasing false alarms across the yard.
The welts are the flashlights. The itch is the panic button. The migration is the security camera swiveling around like it saw a ghost.
Sometimes there is a real threat. Sometimes the system just hates Tuesday.
The goal is to identify triggers, calm the reaction, and know when swelling or breathing symptoms mean emergency.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Sensitivity Dial Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a hives problem. You may also have a hyper-reactive boundary pattern.
Medical first: hives are histamine/mast-cell reactions. Allergies, meds, infections, temperature, pressure, stress, autoimmune issues, and mystery nonsense can all matter.
If breathing, throat, tongue, lips, fainting, or severe systemic symptoms show up, get emergency care.
Symbolically, ask where your alarm system is set too high.
What are you reacting to before you even know if it is a threat? What request, person, room, or obligation makes your skin hit the panic button?
Track the trigger. Lower the load. Set the boundary. Keep the emergency line clear for actual emergencies.
Turn down the sensitivity dial or keep letting the alarm scream at every leaf. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Lung Wei Qi Boundary
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, the Lung and Wei Qi symbolize surface defense.
Hives can symbolically mirror a boundary system that reacts too fast to too much.

influenceLayers.title: Liver Wind

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Insomnia Is Real
body: Insomnia can involve trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, waking too early, or sleep that does not restore you.

The neon sign says OPEN. The O burned out, so now it says PEN, which helps no one.
The dishwasher is a raccoon who only comes out at night. Cortisol does not trust the raccoon. Fair, but still not helpful.
The chairs need to go up. The grill needs to cool. The fryer needs to stop hissing like it pays rent.
The diner does not need another strategy meeting at 3 AM. It needs a closing protocol.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest

What are you waiting to go wrong? What conversation are you replaying? What unfinished danger is your body still clocked in for?
Rest is not laziness. Sleep is not optional decoration. It is maintenance, and your brain is not above maintenance, no matter how dramatic it gets.
Close the diner. Put the chairs up. Tell the sentinel the shift is over.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Psoriasis Is Immune-Mediated
body: Psoriasis is medically an immune-mediated inflammatory skin disease.
It is not dirt, poor hygiene, or your body writing a gothic poem for attention.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Skin Turnover Speeds Up
body: Inflammatory signaling causes skin cells to build faster than they shed.
That can create thick plaques, silvery scale, redness, itching, pain, cracking, or bleeding.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Triggers and Treatment
body: Flares may be affected by stress, infection, skin injury, weather, medications, smoking, alcohol, or other triggers.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty

paragraphs: Your skin is a construction site where the foreman keeps screaming MORE BRICKS.
The keratinocytes are bricklayers working triple shifts. Nobody approved this schedule. OSHA has questions.
Normally, skin builds and sheds in an orderly rhythm. Here, the crew panic-builds plaques like a fortress around a house that may not even be under attack.
The excess bricks become scale. The wall gets thicker. The foreman calls it safety. The tenants call it itchy.
The sign says WE'RE DOING OUR BEST. Someone wrote PLEASE STOP BUILDING THE WALL underneath.
The fix is not shame. It is immune care, trigger tracking, skin support, and checking whether the joints are joining the nonsense.

What hurt taught you to build layers instead of boundaries? Who made softness feel unsafe? What relationship with visibility makes you want armor instead of skin?

Care for the plaques. Track triggers. Get the joints checked if they complain. Then stop mistaking isolation for safety.
Stop overbuilding the wall or keep paying the bricklayers in skin. Your choice.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Fungus, Not Worm
body: Ringworm is medically a contagious fungal skin infection.

body: It can spread through infected people, animals, contaminated clothing, towels, surfaces, or shared equipment.
It often appears as an itchy, scaly, circular or ring-like patch.

Steroid creams without antifungal treatment can worsen or disguise fungal infection, because the body loves trap doors.

If it changes, bleeds, itches, hurts, grows, has uneven color, jagged borders, or looks unlike your other moles, get it checked.
Symbolically, ask what in your life feels permanent just because it has been there a long time.
Not every mark is a destiny. Not every old label is true. But some marks need biopsy before philosophy.
Update the museum labels. Change what can change. Check what must be checked.
Update the museum labels or keep pretending every exhibit is permanent. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Liver Blood Marker
paragraphs: In symbolic Chinese medicine language, fixed marks can be read as stagnation or memory.
Medically, they are pigment-cell clusters. The symbol does not replace the skin check.

influenceLayers.title: Permanent Marker Archetype
paragraphs: The mole can symbolize an old marker of identity.
The question is whether the mark is stable, changing, or asking for attention.

influenceLayers.title: The Museum Exhibit
paragraphs: Some exhibits belong in the permanent collection.
Some need updated labels. Some need a dermatologist with a dermatoscope.

influenceLayers.title: Ugly Duckling Signal
paragraphs: A mark that stands out from the rest deserves respect.
Different can be meaningful. It can also be medical.

influenceLayers.title: Inspection Before Interpretation
paragraphs: The reset is observe, photograph if useful, compare, and get checked when suspicious.
Then process the meaning without ignoring the body.

reset.title: Marker Check & Change Permission Reset
steps: Do not poke, scrape, or pick at the mole.
Look calmly and note whether it has changed.
Inhale for 4 seconds and exhale for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I can change what is ready to change, and I can check what needs checking.
If it is suspicious, schedule dermatology instead of negotiating with Google Images.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a substitute for skin cancer screening. Suspicious moles need medical evaluation.

items.label: ABCDE check
items.detail: Watch asymmetry, border irregularity, color variation, diameter/growth, and evolution.

items.label: Ugly duckling
items.detail: A mole that looks different from your others should be checked.

items.label: Sun protection
items.detail: Sun protection supports skin health and reduces UV exposure.

items.label: Photo tracking
items.detail: Photos can help track change over time, but they do not replace dermatology.

avoid: Do not ignore changing, bleeding, painful, itchy, irregular, new, or ugly-duckling moles.
Do not remove moles at home.

brutalActions: Check ABCDE.
Book dermatology for suspicious changes.
Do not pick or home-remove.
Retire one old identity label that only stayed because nobody updated the exhibit.

clinical.mechanism: Moles, or nevi, are clusters of pigment-producing cells. Many are benign, but changing, irregular, bleeding, itching, painful, new, or unusual moles need medical/dermatology evaluation to rule out melanoma or other skin cancer.
protocol: Use ABCDE and ugly-duckling checks.
Track changes with photos if helpful.
Seek dermatology care for changing, bleeding, painful, itchy, irregular, new, or unusual moles.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for dermatology evaluation.

witty.metaphorText: Your skin is a museum where most exhibits are boring, but changing exhibits need an actual inspector.
witty.wittyAdvice: Do not let a suspicious mole cosplay as a metaphor.

brutal.realityCheck: You may have a mole concern. You may also have a fixed-marker pattern.

critical_alerts: Asymmetry, irregular border, multiple colors, rapid growth, or evolution/change
Bleeding, crusting, itching, pain, ulceration, or non-healing spot
New mole or lesion that looks different from the others
Very dark, blue-black, red, white, or unevenly colored lesion
Personal/family history of melanoma or many atypical moles
Changing lesion on scalp, nails, palms, soles, genitals, or hard-to-see areas
seek_immediate_care_if: A mole is rapidly changing, bleeding, painful, ulcerated, or very suspicious
A lesion looks like melanoma or is dramatically different from your other moles
You have concerning skin cancer history and a changing lesion
schedule_care_if: Any mole changes in size, shape, color, border, sensation, or behavior
You have many moles, atypical moles, or family/personal melanoma history
You are unsure whether a mole is normal
medical_safety.self_care_allowed_if: Stable moles can be monitored with sun protection and routine skin awareness, but suspicious changes need evaluation.
medical_safety.do_not_ignore: Moles are not merely emotional markers. Changing or unusual lesions can be skin cancer warning signs.
medical_safety.disclaimer: This app is for education and self-reflection only. It does not diagnose, treat, prevent, or cure disease.
